prompt: I want to trace my DSPy pipeline so I can see all the LM calls, prompts, and token counts. What is the easiest way to set this up?
expected_output: Sets up Langtrace with langtrace.init() for automatic DSPy tracing
assertions: Calls langtrace.init() before importing or configuring DSPy
Sets API key via LANGTRACE_API_KEY env var or api_key parameter
Does NOT add decorators to individual DSPy modules — auto-instrumentation handles it
Mentions what gets traced: LM calls, token counts, cost, latency

prompt: I want to run Langtrace on my own infrastructure for data privacy. How do I self-host it?
expected_output: Shows Docker Compose self-hosted setup with api_host pointing to local instance
assertions: Clones the Langtrace repo from GitHub
Uses docker compose up to start the self-hosted instance
Configures langtrace.init() with api_host pointing to localhost
Does NOT require a cloud API key for self-hosted setup

prompt: I want to add metadata like user_id and environment to my DSPy traces so I can filter them in production. How?
assertions: Uses @with_langtrace_root_span decorator to create a named parent span
Calls langtrace.inject_additional_attributes() with a dictionary of metadata
Shows filtering by metadata in the Langtrace UI
